report.query: Research profitable quantitative trading approaches for GBPUSD under ranging conditions.
report.summary: Consensus (semantic_scholar): These indicators represented the supply of 18 NCP across 12 ecosystems, both inside and outside Kilimanjaro National Park. This dissertation demonstrates the value of an interdisciplinary approach for sustainability in a complex SES of Kilimanjaro, Tanzania. In the second chapter, I assessed multifunctionality, i.e., the measure of NCP supply relative to demand under changes in NCP access, NCP demand, and land use. These demand changes interacted with land-use change; e.g., switches from cropping to traditional agroforestry under traditional-lifestyle demand increased multifunctionality for most actors. Our approach reveals the sensitivity of a SES to both social and ecological drivers and demonstrates a quantitative, holistic decision-support approach for SES transformation.

consensus.claim: These indicators represented the supply of 18 NCP across 12 ecosystems, both inside and outside Kilimanjaro National Park.

report.agreement_narrative: Consensus (semantic_scholar): These indicators represented the supply of 18 NCP across 12 ecosystems, both inside and outside Kilimanjaro National Park.
findings: Gathered 1 items across 1 independent sources over 3 round(s).

evidence.title: Understanding the multidimensionality of the Kilimanjaro social-ecological system: insights for building desirable futures
evidence.text: Worldwide social–ecological systems (SES) face growing social and environmental pressures that threaten their sustainability—that is, their ability to support a high quality of life for people while maintaining ecological processes. As a concept, SES describes the interconnected social and ecological components in which different forms of knowledge (scientific, local, and experiential) and rules (both formal and informal) influence human needs. Ecosystems provide beneficial Nature’s Contributions to People (NCP), including regulating (e.g., pollination), material (e.g., food), and non-material NCP (e.g., cultural benefits). Because SES are complex and shaped by local conditions and feedback
evidence.author: N. Kinabo

insights: Generalization: moderate overfitting (OOS significantly below IS)
directives: Seed future populations from this champion's DNA
Store winning module combination in Chronicle
reflection.conclusion: The winning strategy uses hma_slope trend detection with adx_strength momentum confirmation and atr_expansion volatility filtering. It achieved 0.0445 return over 12 OOS trades (Sharpe=0.305), indicating moderate overfitting (OOS significantly below IS). This genome should serve as the reference for future trending_up evolution.
